decision_point: In an adult with primary frozen shoulder (adhesive capsulitis), is early structured physical therapy or surgical intervention (MUA or arthroscopic capsular release) preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with primary frozen shoulder (adhesive capsulitis), is early structured physical therapy or surgical intervention (MUA or arthroscopic capsular release) preferred?
Options:
  1. Structured physical therapy
  2. Surgical intervention
label: settled
label_rationale: The UK FROST trial found no patient-reported outcome advantage at 12 months for MUA or arthroscopic capsular release over early structured physical therapy; nonoperative care is first-line, surgery reserved for refractory cases. Settled conservative-first standard (MD adjudication 2026-06-27; reproducibly converged at N=3).
